page: Amazon Web Services g4ad.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring AMD EPYC 7R32 processors with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The graphics subsystem consists of four AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs providing a total of 32 GB of VRAM.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in specific areas, particularly in ray tracing, passmark CPU extended instructions, encryption, and database operations, all ranking in the top 25%. However, single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is in the bottom 10%, while multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and web serving throughput remain in the middle 50%. Large-scale LLM inference is relatively weak, landing in the bottom 25% for 70B models.

From a resource density perspective, the g4ad.16xlarge offers a balanced profile combining dedicated AMD CPU cores, local NVMe storage, and multiple AMD GPUs. The primary tradeoff lies in its single-core processing limitations, which may bottleneck serial tasks, whereas parallelized workloads benefit from the multi-GPU and multi-core architecture.

This instance is suited for graphics-intensive applications, ray tracing, database operations, and moderately sized machine learning inference tasks. It is also applicable for workloads requiring high-speed local NVMe storage and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 256 GB of DDR4 memory. It features four AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs with 32 GB of total VRAM and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong performance in ray tracing, database operations, and encryption, though single-core CPU performance is relatively weak. This hardware profile makes the instance highly suitable for graphics-intensive workloads, ray tracing, database management, and parallelized compute tasks that benefit from multi-GPU acceleration and fast local storage.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring AMD EPYC processors, four Radeon Pro GPUs, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for parallel workloads.
